ip_outcome_note: Card oracle text names 'Anax' explicitly; type line is Legendary Enchantment Creature — Demigod; extensive named-character lore for king of Akros on Theros. Original printing is THB-125 (January 2020). Wizards-internal IP — no external licensing risk.

trivia_bullets.text: CMM no. 204 (August 4, 2023) is a reprint of Theros Beyond Death THB-125 (January 24, 2020). Commander Masters was a reprint-focused set with no new story content, designed to increase access to powerful Commander cards.

Anax was king of Akros on Theros, originally a follower of Iroas (god of victory), who later turned to Purphoros (god of the forge) as he built a small empire. He was gravely wounded in battle with Rhordon the Rageblood during Xenagos's siege of Akros. His wife Queen Cymede was claimed by Keranos and ascended to Nyx as divine lightning. In Theros Beyond Death, Anax returned as a Nyxborn demigod, leading satyr warriors and wielding a molten, smoke-wreathed axe.
sources: CoolStuffInc: Legends of Theros, Jay Annelli (2019)

trivia_bullets.text: Theros Beyond Death introduced five demigod uncommon legendary creatures, all with devotion-based power/toughness and a triggered ability producing tokens on creature death. Anax was the red demigod in this cycle. The Enchantment creature type reflects the Theros mechanic: gods and demigods are enchantments first, becoming creatures when sufficiently worshipped (here: through devotion to red).
sources: dotesports.com: MTG guide for every demigod in Theros: Beyond Death

trivia_bullets.text: EDHREC rank 6,677 with over 376 Commander decks using Anax as head commander, primarily in Mono-Red Devotion, Aristocrats, and Sacrifice archetypes. The token-on-death trigger is notably broad — any nontoken creature you control dying creates at least one Satyr, giving red persistent board presence through sweepers.

related_cards.name: The Triumph of Anax

related_cards.name: Purphoros, God of the Forge
